meaning: adj.不足取的,无价值的,琐碎的,下贱的
examples: He received a paltry annuity of $100.
This account offers a paltry 1% return on your investment.
Ye paltry cowards, and arm yourselves and guard the doors, whilst I send one to fetch the watch!
Those new purchasers would have to buy the debt no matter how paltry its yield.
The not-so-bold assumption: everyone else will grab just a paltry share of the tablet market.
